response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack software developer with strong web, backend, and some low-latency systems experience, but the resume shows no evidence of the core requirements for this role: SoC/IP modeling, hardware performance architecture, AI accelerator or GPU workload analysis, or hardware-software co-design. While he meets the years-of-experience bar (BS + 15+ years) and has touched performance-sensitive code (DLang async I/O at Dunnhumby) and embedded systems tangentially, the domain gap between web/product engineering and principal-level silicon performance architecture is too large to bridge in an interview process.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded contexts
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, caching, fibers in DLang)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and community teaching
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, or silicon/hardware architecture experience anywhere in the resume
No deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator performance analysis background
Career trajectory is web/product full-stack, not principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware co-design experience
No AI/DL workload or GPU performance analysis background
